novel_features.name: Tour-routing feasibility
novel_features.description: Find which tracked artists already have shows near a target city within a date window — feasibility-ranked routing candidates for booking.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local events × venues × tracked watchlist with haversine distance and date-window math. No Bandsintown endpoint exposes 'who's playing near X between Y and Z'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When booking an event in a specific city, the cheapest path is finding an artist already routing through the region. Reach for this before any cold outreach.

novel_features.name: Calendar-gap detection
novel_features.description: Find empty windows in an artist's tour calendar that match an event slot length, optionally constrained to a region.
novel_features.rationale: Uses SQL window functions over an artist's synced events to compute gaps between consecutive shows. Impossible from a stateless API call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to identify artists with a touring gap that aligns with your event date — the most actionable booking lead.

novel_features.name: Lineup co-bill mining
novel_features.description: Surface which artists frequently co-bill with a given artist by aggregating lineup arrays across many events.
novel_features.rationale: Mines the lineup[] array from every event in the local store. Bandsintown returns lineups one event at a time with no aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When building festival lineups, find natural co-bill pairings backed by real shared-stage history.

novel_features.name: Tracker trend
novel_features.description: Track tracker_count and upcoming_event_count over time per artist; surface rising and falling demand signals.
novel_features.rationale: Bandsintown returns tracker_count as a scalar — there is no history endpoint. Local snapshot table turns it into a time-series with LAG() deltas.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Rising tracker_count is a leading indicator of audience demand; use to decide who to book before peers notice.

novel_features.name: Idempotent pull with diff
novel_features.description: Re-fetch every tracked artist's events with a staleness window; emit a structured diff (added / removed / changed events) for downstream agents.
novel_features.rationale: Bandsintown has no cursor or webhook. This command synthesizes a cursor from staleness windows and emits agent-native diffs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this in a daily cron; pipe the diff into Slack or a project tracker to alert when tracked artists add new shows.

novel_features.name: SEA routing radar
novel_features.description: One-shot Southeast Asia briefing: all upcoming shows in a date range across tracked artists, grouped by city, tagged with tracker tier.
novel_features.rationale: Composed query joining events × tracked watchlist × tracker_count percentile, scoped to a SEA bounding box. Single command replaces an hour of manual artist-by-artist scanning.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Monday-morning briefing in one invocation: who is touring SEA in your event window, ranked by demand.

novel_features.name: Watchlist primitive
novel_features.description: Local watchlist of artists you care about. Drives sync, snapshot, route, and sea-radar.
novel_features.rationale: Bandsintown has no notion of 'my tracked artists' — this is a first-class local table the CLI introduces.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Build a curated watchlist once; every other intelligence command reads from it.

narrative.headline: Turn Bandsintown's two read-only endpoints into a tour-routing brain: local cache, calendar-gap detection, lineup co-bill mining, and tracker-count trends.
narrative.value_prop: Every existing Bandsintown wrapper is a 10-year-old language binding for the same two API endpoints. This CLI absorbs all of them and adds the queries promoters and tour routers actually need: feasibility-ranked routing candidates for a target city and date, empty windows in an artist's calendar, co-bill patterns across past festivals, and tracker_count trends over time. Local SQLite store, FTS search, agent-native --json output, and MCP exposure are standard.
narrative.auth_narrative: Bandsintown's API is partner-only as of 2025 — a free 'pick any app_id' string no longer works. Apply for partner access at help.bandsintown.com, then set BANDSINTOWN_APP_ID in your environment.

quickstart.comment: Set BANDSINTOWN_APP_ID in your environment first (apply for a partner key at help.bandsintown.com). Then verify auth and reachability.
Build a local watchlist; every routing query reads from it
Pull every tracked artist's events into the local store
Find routing candidates for your target Jakarta date

troubleshoots.fix: Your BANDSINTOWN_APP_ID is missing, expired, or not a partner-issued key. Apply at help.bandsintown.com — the free app_id model was deprecated.

troubleshoots.symptom: Artist name with special chars returns 404
troubleshoots.fix: The CLI double-escapes /, ?, *, and " per the spec. If you're hitting 404, check the artist exists on bandsintown.com first.
Run `bandsintown-pp-cli pull --tracked` first to populate the local store, and check `track list` is non-empty.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when a booking question depends on aggregating Bandsintown data across artists or over time — tour-routing feasibility, calendar-gap detection, co-bill patterns, tracker_count deltas. For one-off 'what shows does Phoenix have' queries, the bandsintown.com web UI is fine. For repeatable, agent-driven, multi-artist intelligence, this is the right tool.

recipes.title: Monday-morning SEA routing briefing
recipes.explanation: Print a SEA-only briefing for the August event window with only the fields you need. Run `bandsintown-pp-cli pull --tracked` first to hydrate the local store.

recipes.title: Rising-demand artists this month
recipes.explanation: Compare today's tracker_count snapshot against snapshots from the last 30 days. Output the top 20 rising artists. Run `bandsintown-pp-cli snapshot` more than once over time to grow the time series.

recipes.title: Festival co-bill for a candidate headliner
recipes.explanation: Surface every artist who shared a stage with Phoenix on 2+ events since 2024. Pipes cleanly into your lineup planner.

recipes.title: Find a gap in an artist's calendar that fits your event
recipes.explanation: Show empty windows of 5–21 days in Beach House's SEA touring calendar — the actionable booking leads.

trigger_phrases: is artist X touring near jakarta
find routing candidates for a jakarta show
who's playing southeast asia in august
